project.name: powerlifting-coach
project.description: IRON LEDGER — AI-powered powerlifting coach with block periodization, CV-based form checking, and nutrition planning

nodes.name: ChatView.tsx
nodes.summary: React client component that renders the full chat UI with streaming AI responses, quick-action chips, and auto-scroll behavior for the coaching conversation interface.

nodes.name: ChatView
nodes.summary: Exported React component managing chat state, streaming SSE from the /api/chat endpoint, and rendering messages with quick-send chips and an auto-scrolling message list.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js server page component for the /chat route that authenticates the session, fetches the last 200 chat messages from SQLite, and renders the ChatView client component.

nodes.name: ChatPage
nodes.summary: Async server component that requires an authenticated session, loads chat history from the database, and passes it as initial messages to ChatView.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handling POST requests for AI coaching chat: builds rich athlete context from profile, program, and form checks, then streams the AI response and persists both messages to SQLite.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates session, assembles rich athlete context, saves the user message, initiates an AI streaming response, and persists the assistant reply on stream completion.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route for video-based form analysis: receives a multipart video upload, dispatches it to the Python CV sidecar, estimates RPE from velocity data, calls the AI for structured feedback, and persists the form check and velocity log records.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles multipart/form-data for form checking: validates lift type, calls CV sidecar for pose analysis, retrieves velocity calibration history, estimates RPE, generates AI feedback, and writes form_checks and velocity_log rows.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route that computes macro targets from the athlete profile and returns an AI-generated meal plan as structured JSON.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Loads the athlete profile, computes macros via macroTargets, calls aiGenerate with the nutrition system prompt, and returns parsed meal plan JSON.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route that validates and persists an athlete's onboarding profile, estimates novice maxes if needed, and generates and stores their first training program via AI.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates the incoming profile with Zod, applies novice max estimation if no lifts provided, upserts the athlete row, calls generateProgram, and inserts the initial program record.

nodes.name: generateProgram
nodes.summary: Calls aiGenerate with the program system prompt, parses the returned JSON, and returns a structured block-periodization program; retries once if parsing fails.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route that algorithmically adjusts the current training week's exercises based on the athlete's recent session logs and RPE targets, without an additional AI call.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Loads the program and recent session logs, extracts per-lift sets, and applies adjustExercise from programming.ts to update exercise weights for the current week, returning the modified program.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route that regenerates the athlete's training program from scratch using the AI model, replacing the existing program record in the database.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Fetches athlete profile, calls aiGenerate with the program prompt, parses and validates the resulting week array, then inserts a new program record.

nodes.name: ai.ts
nodes.summary: Multi-provider AI abstraction layer supporting Anthropic and Google Gemini backends; exposes unified aiGenerate and aiStream functions, provider detection via LLM_PROVIDER environment variable, and JSON utility helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses lazy singleton pattern for clients; provider is determined at runtime via LLM_PROVIDER env var, defaulting to Anthropic.

nodes.name: aiGenerate
nodes.summary: Exported facade that routes to anthropicGenerate or geminiGenerate based on the active provider and returns the full text response string.

nodes.name: isAiKeyError
nodes.summary: Detects whether an error is caused by a missing or invalid API key so callers can surface a user-friendly configuration message.

nodes.name: safeParseJson
nodes.summary: Strips markdown code fences from AI output then parses as JSON, returning null on failure rather than throwing.

nodes.name: anthropicGenerate
nodes.summary: Calls the Anthropic messages API with the provided options and returns the concatenated text content.

nodes.name: geminiGenerate
nodes.summary: Calls the Gemini generateContent API and returns the trimmed text response.

nodes.name: cvService.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the Python FastAPI computer-vision sidecar service; uploads video files for pose extraction and returns structured per-rep bar-path and velocity data.
nodes.languageNotes: CvServiceError extends Error with a status code field for clean HTTP error propagation to callers.

nodes.name: analyzeVideo
nodes.summary: Sends a multipart video file to the CV sidecar endpoint, handles non-2xx responses as CvServiceError, validates the response schema, and returns typed CvResult data.

nodes.name: CvServiceError
nodes.summary: Custom error class carrying the HTTP status code returned by the CV sidecar, enabling callers to distinguish service errors from network errors.

nodes.name: programming.ts
nodes.summary: Core algorithmic programming library implementing e1RM estimation, weight suggestion, RPE-based exercise adjustment, session log extraction, and block periodization stage planning for powerlifting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RPE percentage tables from calculations.ts; all weight arithmetic respects equipment unit (kg vs lb) via plateStep/roundToStep.

nodes.name: adjustExercise
nodes.summary: Adjusts a planned exercise's weight and RPE based on recent session history: advances load on success, holds or deloads on failure, and respects equipment unit rounding.

nodes.name: extractLifterSets
nodes.summary: Extracts and enriches all logged sets for a given lift type from session logs, computing e1RM for each set and sorting by date descending.

nodes.name: stagePlan
nodes.summary: Returns a block periodization stage configuration (accumulation, intensification, or peaking) appropriate for the athlete's experience level.

nodes.name: chat.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the system prompt for the AI coaching chat, injecting the athlete profile, current training week, and recent form check summaries as context.

nodes.name: buildChatSystemPrompt
nodes.summary: Constructs a detailed system prompt string from athlete profile, program week, and recent form checks to provide the AI coach with full training context.

nodes.name: formcheck.ts
nodes.summary: Prompt builder for AI form analysis: generates per-lift system prompts and constructs user prompts that embed CV bar-path tables, RPE estimates, and athlete context for bench, squat, and deadlift analysis.

nodes.name: buildFormCheckPrompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the user-turn prompt for form analysis including lift-specific CV data tables, pose coverage, warnings, and athlete cue preferences.

nodes.name: systemPromptFor
nodes.summary: Returns the appropriate lift-specific system prompt constant for the given lift type, falling back to the generic default.

nodes.name: nutrition.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the nutrition AI system prompt constant and builds the user-turn prompt embedding athlete macros, bodyweight, and dietary restrictions for meal plan generation.

nodes.name: buildNutritionUserPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds the user prompt for nutrition plan generation, injecting macro targets, bodyweight, training phase, and dietary restrictions.

nodes.name: program.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the training program AI system prompt and builds the user prompt that embeds the athlete's current 1RMs, experience, stage plan, and injury notes for block periodization generation.

nodes.name: buildProgramUserPrompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the AI user prompt for program generation, incorporating the athlete's 1RMs, experience stage plan, injuries, and equipment preference.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Central TypeScript type definitions and Zod schemas for the entire application, covering athlete profiles, training programs, form checks, nutrition plans, CV results, and chat messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines TypeScript interfaces with Zod runtime validators; many types are inferred from Zod schemas via z.infer<> for DRY type safety across the full stack.

nodes.name: velocity.ts
nodes.summary: Implements velocity-based RPE estimation using per-lift velocity loss curves, optional per-athlete linear regression calibration, and RIR lookup tables for honest effort quantification.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses linear regression (linFit) for per-lifter calibration when sufficient history is available, otherwise falls back to generic lift-specific loss curves.

nodes.name: estimateRpe
nodes.summary: Primary exported function estimating RPE from velocity loss percentage, using per-lifter calibrated linear regression when history is available and generic curves otherwise.

nodes.name: rirFromLoss
nodes.summary: Maps a velocity loss percentage to reps-in-reserve for a given lift type using lift-specific lookup curves.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router layout for the authenticated app shell â€” checks session and redirects unauthenticated users before rendering the AppShell wrapper.

nodes.name: LogSessionModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal component that lets athletes log actual weights, reps, and RPE for a training day's exercises, submitting the session data to the API and refreshing the router on success.

nodes.name: LogSessionModal
nodes.summary: Renders a full-screen modal form for recording set-by-set performance data for a program day, managing per-set weight/reps/RPE state and submitting to the session logging API.

nodes.name: ProgramView.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary program display component rendering the athlete's weekly training schedule with week tabs, expandable day cards, exercise rows with AI-adjusted weights, and a session logging trigger.

nodes.name: ProgramView
nodes.summary: Top-level program view component that fetches AI weight adjustments per week, renders week navigation tabs, and orchestrates DayCard and LogSessionModal rendering.

nodes.name: DayCard
nodes.summary: Collapsible card for a single training day, listing its exercises and showing the log session button with lift-type indicator.

nodes.name: ExerciseRow
nodes.summary: Renders a single exercise entry with prescribed sets/reps/RPE alongside AI-adjusted weight targets and any modification notes.

nodes.name: guessLiftType
nodes.summary: Infers the primary lift category (squat, bench, deadlift) from a training day's exercise names via keyword matching.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server component page that requires authentication, fetches the athlete's profile and most recent program from SQLite, then renders ProgramView with the structured data.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Login page providing an email/name form that authenticates or registers an athlete via the auth API, then redirects to the dashboard on success.

nodes.name: OnboardingWizard.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-step onboarding wizard collecting athlete profile data across five steps â€” personal info, strength maxes, training preferences, dietary restrictions, and a confirmation summary â€” before submitting to the profile API.

nodes.name: OnboardingWizard
nodes.summary: Root wizard component managing step navigation, cumulative profile state, and final submission of the completed athlete profile to the API.

nodes.name: Step1
nodes.summary: First onboarding step collecting the athlete's basic info â€” age, sex, height, weight, bodyweight unit, and experience level.

nodes.name: Step2
nodes.summary: Second onboarding step capturing squat, bench, and deadlift one-rep maxes plus training frequency and preferred weight unit.

nodes.name: Step3
nodes.summary: Third onboarding step gathering training schedule preferences including days per week, available equipment, and injury history.

nodes.name: Step4
nodes.summary: Fourth onboarding step collecting the athlete's primary goal, recovery capacity, sleep hours, and dietary restriction toggles.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server component page for the onboarding route â€” redirects already-authenticated users away and renders the OnboardingWizard for new athletes.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Public landing page that redirects authenticated users to the dashboard and presents the IRON LEDGER marketing hero section with feature highlights for new visitors.

nodes.name: AppShell.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level authenticated layout shell composing SideNav and MobileNav around the main content area with responsive sidebar and bottom navigation.

nodes.name: MobileNav.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom navigation bar for mobile viewports with primary route links and a slide-up sheet for secondary items, using active-path highlighting.

nodes.name: MobileNav
nodes.summary: Renders a sticky bottom nav bar with primary and secondary navigation links, expanding a slide-up sheet for overflow items on mobile screens.

nodes.name: SideNav.tsx
nodes.summary: Sidebar navigation for desktop viewports rendering the LiftlyLogo and a vertical list of app links with active-state styling.

nodes.name: SideNav
nodes.summary: Renders a fixed left-side navigation panel with logo and app route links, highlighting the active route using pathname prefix matching.

nodes.name: Button.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable button component with variant (default, outline, ghost, danger) and size props, built with Tailwind class merging for the dark design system.

nodes.name: Input.tsx
nodes.summary: Styled form primitives â€” Input, Select, and Textarea â€” with consistent dark-themed Tailwind classes for use across the app's forms.

nodes.name: LiftlyLogo.tsx
nodes.summary: Brand logo component rendering the IRON LEDGER barbell SVG icon and optional wordmark, with configurable size and className props.

nodes.name: LiftlyLogo
nodes.summary: Renders the branded barbell SVG mark alongside an optional wordmark, scaling via the size prop and merging additional className overrides.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Shared utility module exporting a Tailwind class merger (cn), a weight formatter with kg/lb conversion, and a locale-aware date formatter.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges Tailwind class strings using clsx and tailwind-merge to resolve conflicts and strip duplicates.

nodes.name: formatWeight
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric weight value with its unit label, converting kg to lbs when the unit is imperial.

nodes.name: fmtDate
nodes.summary: Formats a date string into a locale-friendly short display string for use in session logs and program history.

nodes.name: Card.tsx
nodes.summary: Chalk-card surface primitives (Card + header/section helpers) for the dark theme.

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: Renders a styled dark card container accepting arbitrary className and children props.

nodes.name: CardHeader
nodes.summary: Renders a card header row with optional title, subtitle, and accent colour stripe.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard route summarizing the current training week, recent sessions, and quick actions.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: Async server component that fetches athlete profile, current program, session logs, bodyweight, and weekly tonnage, then renders the full dashboard layout.

nodes.name: FormCheckClient.tsx
nodes.summary: Form-check client: records/uploads a bench clip, posts to the form-check API, and renders the annotated bar path, rep metrics, RPE estimate, and coaching.

nodes.name: FormCheckClient
nodes.summary: Root client component managing form-check list state and toggling the upload modal; exported for use in the server page.

nodes.name: FormCheckCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single form-check result with CV warnings, POSE bar-path coverage, rep table, phase findings, working-well summary, and inline RPE calibration controls.

nodes.name: LogAsSessionControl
nodes.summary: UI control that POSTs a form-check result to the session-log API and shows loading/success/error feedback.

nodes.name: CalibrateControl
nodes.summary: Inline RPE calibration widget that lets the athlete override the AI-estimated RPE and PATCHes the update to the form-check API.

nodes.name: UploadModal
nodes.summary: Full-screen modal for uploading a lift video: collects lift type, stance, load, unit, and optional context, then POSTs multipart/form-data to the CV analysis endpoint.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Form-check route wrapper rendering the upload-and-analyze client.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Creates a session log directly from a form-check result, carrying over the measured RPE.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests by validating the body with Zod, fetching the form-check and athlete profile, converting units, and inserting a new session_log row.

nodes.name: NutritionView.tsx
nodes.summary: Nutrition view showing deterministic macro targets and AI-generated meal plans honoring dietary restrictions.

nodes.name: NutritionView
nodes.summary: Renders the full nutrition dashboard with macro targets, a generate button that calls the AI meal-plan API, and displays the returned plan with meals and supplements.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Nutrition route wrapper rendering computed macro targets and meal plans.

nodes.name: PlateSpinner.tsx
nodes.summary: Rotating plate loading indicator used during long AI generation calls.

nodes.name: ProgressView.tsx
nodes.summary: Recharts dashboards for e1RM trend, bodyweight rolling average, and weekly tonnage per lift.

nodes.name: ProgressView
nodes.summary: Renders tabbed Recharts charts for squat/bench/deadlift e1RM history, bodyweight trend with 7-day rolling average, and weekly tonnage per lift.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Progress route loading session history and rendering the charts view.

nodes.name: ProgressPage
nodes.summary: Async server component that runs multiple SQLite queries to build e1RM time-series and weekly tonnage data structures before rendering ProgressView.

nodes.name: RPEBadge.tsx
nodes.summary: Colored badge rendering an RPE value using the rpeColor scale.

nodes.name: RPEBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a pill badge whose background colour transitions from green to red based on the RPE value using rpeColor() from calculations.

nodes.name: calculations.ts
nodes.summary: Pure sports-science math: unit conversions, BMR/TDEE/macro targets, e1RM (Brzycki/Epley), and the RPE to %1RM lookup used across nutrition and programming.

nodes.name: macroTargets
nodes.summary: Computes protein/fat/carb targets from TDEE and phase adjustment, honoring the athlete goal.

nodes.name: estimatedOneRM
nodes.summary: Estimates 1RM from a weight-by-reps set, blending Brzycki and Epley for higher reps.

nodes.name: epley
nodes.summary: Implements the Epley 1RM formula: weight * (1 + reps/30).

nodes.name: brzycki
nodes.summary: Implements the Brzycki 1RM formula: weight * (36 / (37 - reps)).

nodes.name: rpePercent
nodes.summary: RPE + reps to percentage of 1RM via the standard RPE chart.

nodes.name: suggestWeight
nodes.summary: Suggests a working weight for a given oneRM, rep count, and target RPE, rounding to the nearest 2.5 kg or 5 lb.

nodes.name: noviceMaxEstimate
nodes.summary: Estimates starting maxes for novices from profile data when no logged sets exist yet.

nodes.name: mifflin
nodes.summary: Computes BMR using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation with sex-specific constants.

nodes.name: katch
nodes.summary: Computes BMR using the Katch-McArdle equation based on lean body mass when body-fat percentage is available.

nodes.name: bmr
nodes.summary: Basal metabolic rate via Mifflin-St Jeor or Katch-McArdle depending on available body-fat data.

nodes.name: tdee
nodes.summary: Total daily energy expenditure from BMR scaled by training-day activity multiplier.

nodes.name: parseHeightCm
nodes.summary: Parses a height string in either centimetres or feet-and-inches format (e.g., "5'10") and returns the value in centimetres.

nodes.name: rpeColor
nodes.summary: Maps an RPE value to a Tailwind background-colour class, graduating from green at low effort to red at maximal effort.

nodes.name: init-db.ts
nodes.summary: One-shot database initialization script that imports getDb to trigger schema creation and WAL mode setup on first run.

nodes.name: ProfileView.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side React component rendering the full athlete profile editor with fields for name, bodyweight, height, training age, lift maxes, and goals; handles save and AI plan regeneration via API calls.

nodes.name: ProfileView
nodes.summary: Exported React component that manages local draft state for the athlete profile, calls /api/onboarding to save changes, and supports regenerating the AI training plan.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js server component for the profile page that enforces authentication via requireSession, fetches athlete profile JSON from the database, and renders the ProfileView client component.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handling email-only login by validating the request body with Zod, upserting the athlete record, and writing a session cookie.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/auth/login â€” validates email input, calls getOrCreateAthlete, then sets a session cookie and returns the athlete record.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Next.js API route that clears the session cookie and redirects to the login page on POST.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/auth/logout â€” calls clearSession to delete the auth cookie and issues a redirect response.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route that records athlete-supplied actual RPE for a completed form-check set in velocity_log, enabling per-lifter RPE calibration based on rep-time slowdown.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates session and request body, updates the actual_rpe field on the matching velocity_log row, and returns the cumulative calibration count for the lift type.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route that persists a completed training session to session_logs and optionally upserts the athlete's daily bodyweight into bodyweight_logs.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Requires auth, validates and inserts a session log row with exercises JSON, and conditionally upserts a bodyweight log row for the session date.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Core authentication library providing cookie-based session management (set, get, clear) and athlete upsert logic; requireSession enforces authentication on server components and API routes.

nodes.name: getOrCreateAthlete
nodes.summary: Looks up an athlete by normalized email; if not found, inserts a new athlete row with a UUID and returns the record.

nodes.name: setSession
nodes.summary: Writes the athlete ID into an HTTP-only session cookie using Next.js cookies().

nodes.name: clearSession
nodes.summary: Deletes the session cookie to log the athlete out.

nodes.name: getSession
nodes.summary: Reads the session cookie, validates the athlete ID against the database, and returns the full athlete record or null if the session is invalid.

nodes.name: requireSession
nodes.summary: Calls getSession and throws a redirect to /login if no valid session exists, acting as an auth guard for server components and API routes.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Singleton better-sqlite3 database module that opens the SQLite file with WAL mode, runs ensureSchema to create all tables (athletes, sessions, velocity_log, etc.) and applies safe migrations via addColumnIfMissing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses better-sqlite3 synchronous API with WAL mode; includes an in-place table rebuild (relaxVelocityLogMcv) inside a transaction to relax a NOT NULL constraint without dropping the table.

nodes.name: getDb
nodes.summary: Opens or returns the singleton SQLite connection, creating the data directory if absent, enabling WAL mode and foreign keys, then running ensureSchema.

nodes.name: ensureSchema
nodes.summary: Creates all application tables (athletes, form_checks, velocity_log, session_logs, bodyweight_logs, nutrition_plans) using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and applies column migrations.

nodes.name: relaxVelocityLogMcv
nodes.summary: Detects when velocity_log.mcv_ms has a NOT NULL constraint and performs a transactional table rebuild to relax it, preserving all existing rows.

nodes.name: addColumnIfMissing
nodes.summary: Uses PRAGMA table_info to check whether a column exists before issuing ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, making schema migrations idempotent.

nodes.name: uuid
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around crypto.randomUUID() exported for consistent ID generation across the application.

nodes.name: analysis.py
nodes.summary: Core lift analysis engine implementing the Calgary Barbell rep-segmentation model (setup/unrack/descent/pause/press/lockout phases), computing concentric time for velocity-loss-based RPE/RIR estimation, and generating per-lift form coaching notes for bench, squat, and deadlift.

nodes.name: analyze_lift
nodes.summary: Main analysis entry point: segments reps from bar and pose tracks, computes per-rep biomechanical metrics (ROM, joint angles, bar path deviation, concentric time), then delegates to effort and form helpers to produce the complete result payload.

nodes.name: _effort_and_form
nodes.summary: Computes RPE and RIR from rep-time velocity loss across the set, calculates set-level joint stability metrics, dispatches to lift-specific form-note generators, and aggregates the full effort and form summary object.

nodes.name: _bench_notes
nodes.summary: Generates bench-press-specific coaching notes from per-rep metrics, flagging issues such as hip rise, shoulder instability, bar path drift, and foot movement.

nodes.name: _squat_notes
nodes.summary: Generates squat-specific coaching notes from per-rep metrics, identifying depth, hip and knee alignment issues, forward lean, and stance drift.

nodes.name: _deadlift_notes
nodes.summary: Generates deadlift-specific coaching notes from per-rep metrics, flagging back rounding, hip hinge quality, bar drift, and lockout issues.

nodes.name: _render_overlay
nodes.summary: Renders the bar path trajectory as a polyline overlay on a sample frame using OpenCV and returns the result as a base64-encoded JPEG string for embedding in the JSON response.

nodes.name: _rir_from_loss
nodes.summary: Maps a concentric velocity-loss percentage to a Reps-in-Reserve estimate using lift-specific breakpoint tables, implementing the honest RPE calibration model.

nodes.name: _hampel
nodes.summary: Applies a Hampel identifier (median absolute deviation outlier filter) to a 1D signal array to remove spike noise from landmark coordinate timeseries.

nodes.name: _finite_fill
nodes.summary: Replaces NaN/inf values in a per-landmark coordinate array by linear interpolation from surrounding valid detections, handling gaps from missed MediaPipe frames.

nodes.name: _Body
nodes.summary: Lightweight wrapper around a PoseTrack that exposes named joint accessor methods (j, mid, best) for convenient indexed landmark lookups during analysis.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application exposing the /analyze and /health endpoints for the cv-service sidecar; receives uploaded video files, orchestrates pose tracking, bar tracking, and lift analysis, and returns the JSON result to the Next.js caller.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: POST /analyze endpoint handler: validates the uploaded video, writes it to a temp file, calls track_pose and track_bar to build tracks, calls analyze_lift to produce results, cleans up, and returns a sanitized JSONResponse.

nodes.name: _json_safe
nodes.summary: Recursively walks the analysis result tree and sanitizes non-serializable values (NaN, Inf, numpy arrays, numpy scalars) into JSON-safe Python primitives before response serialization.

nodes.name: bar.py
nodes.summary: Tracks bar position across video frames using wrist-landmark seeding plus CSRT optical tracking with Hough circle refinement; returns a BarTrack containing per-frame XY coordinates and plate radius used downstream for path analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cv2.TrackerCSRT with a compatibility shim for OpenCV 4.x vs 3.x API differences; falls back to HoughCircles for per-frame correction when tracker confidence drifts.

nodes.name: _track_bar
nodes.summary: Core bar tracking pipeline: reads all frames, seeds the CSRT tracker from wrist landmarks, tracks forward and backward passes, merges results with Hough circle detections for confident frames, applies sliding-window median smoothing, and validates output quality.

nodes.name: _smooth_enough
nodes.summary: Validates that a candidate bar-path trajectory is spatially smooth by checking frame-to-frame displacement IQR and median step size against plate-radius-normalized thresholds.

nodes.name: _wrist_and_scale
nodes.summary: Extracts wrist pixel coordinates and a pixel-to-meter scale factor from the pose track by measuring the median shoulder-width distance, used to seed the bar tracker and normalize distance measurements.

nodes.name: _hough_near
nodes.summary: Runs cv2.HoughCircles on a cropped grayscale ROI around a given center point to find circular plate candidates within an expected radius range, returning matching circle detections.

nodes.name: diag_pose.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic utility that runs MediaPipe PoseLandmarker on a video at a configurable frame stride, printing landmark coverage and detection quality metrics to stdout; used for offline model tuning and rotation debugging.

nodes.name: diag
nodes.summary: Reads video frames at a given step interval, tests all four rotation codes via the image-mode landmarker, and prints per-rotation detection rates and average landmark visibility to identify the best orientation for a given video file.

nodes.name: make_image_landmarker
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns a MediaPipe PoseLandmarker configured for single-image (non-streaming) inference, used by the diagnostic script to independently evaluate rotation candidates.

nodes.name: download_model.py
nodes.summary: Bootstrap script that downloads the MediaPipe BlazePose task file from the canonical Google storage URL into the cv-service models directory if it is absent or empty.

nodes.name: pose.py
nodes.summary: MediaPipe BlazePose wrapper responsible for reading video frames, auto-detecting the correct rotation code, tracking all pose candidates across frames, selecting the primary lifter track via temporal linking, and returning a PoseTrack with dense per-landmark XY arrays.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MediaPipe Tasks vision API (not the legacy Holistic solution); handles four rotation candidates at startup via a sampled subset of frames to pick the best orientation before full-video tracking.

nodes.name: PoseTrack
nodes.summary: Data container holding the dense landmark coordinate array (xy), visibility scores (vis), FPS, and a sample frame for the selected lifter; exposes coverage() and quality() metrics consumed by analysis.py and app.py.

nodes.name: track_pose
nodes.summary: Public entry point: reads video, determines optimal rotation, runs full-video landmark detection, and calls select_lifter_track to return a PoseTrack for the primary lifter.

nodes.name: select_lifter_track
nodes.summary: Links per-frame pose candidates across time using proximity matching, scores resulting tracks by coverage and horizontal movement, and selects the single best track corresponding to the primary lifter.

nodes.name: _candidate
nodes.summary: Packages a set of MediaPipe landmark results for one detected person into a candidate dict containing pixel coordinates, bounding box, visibility, and geometric heuristics used for lifter identification.

nodes.name: _detect_candidates
nodes.summary: Runs the MediaPipe landmarker on a single frame under a given rotation code and returns a list of candidate dicts built by _candidate, handling coordinate un-rotation.

nodes.name: _best_rotation
nodes.summary: Samples a subset of video frames, evaluates all four rotation codes via _detect_candidates and _lifter_likeness, and returns the rotation code yielding the highest aggregate lifter-likeness score.

nodes.name: _make_landmarker
nodes.summary: Constructs the MediaPipe PoseLandmarker in video-stream mode with heavy model weights and multi-pose detection enabled, verifying the task file exists before instantiation.

nodes.name: _unrotate_xy
nodes.summary: Transforms MediaPipe normalized landmark coordinates back to original-orientation pixel space by applying the inverse of the rotation code applied during detection.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template declaring the six runtime secrets and settings required by IRON LEDGER: LLM provider selection, Gemini and Anthropic API keys, SQLite database path, session secret, and an optional CV service URL for bar-path video analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports a dual-LLM design (GEMINI_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) so operators can switch providers via LLM_PROVIDER without code changes.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project documentation for IRON LEDGER covering the full stack, setup instructions, MVP auth model, annotated file map, and detailed feature notes for program generation, bench bar-path form checking (POSE-based), honest RPE from rep-time slowdown, chat, nutrition, and progress tracking.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js project manifest for the Next.js 14 app, declaring runtime dependencies (Anthropic SDK, better-sqlite3, Recharts, Zod) and dev tooling (TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind CSS), plus npm scripts for dev, build, start, lint, and db:init.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses better-sqlite3 (synchronous SQLite binding) rather than a cloud database, keeping the app fully self-hosted with no external DB dependency.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Next.js 14 project, enabling strict mode, JSX preservation, bundler module resolution, and the @/* path alias mapped to src/, with node_modules excluded.

nodes.name: 135_lb_bench_RPE_RPE_6.5.mp4.json
nodes.summary: CV sidecar evaluation output for the 135 lb bench press sample at RPE 6.5, containing per-frame pose keypoints, bar-path coordinates, per-rep metrics, and a form-quality summary produced by eval_samples.py.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured JSON output with 8 top-level keys: lift, fps, barSource, pose (per-frame), path (bar trajectory), reps, summary, and warnings. All 2210 lines are data (no blank lines).

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion patterns controlling which files the Understand-Anything analyzer scans (data/, env, db files excluded).

nodes.name: gen-batches.cjs
nodes.summary: Phase-2 graph generator that merges deterministic tree-sitter structure with pre-authored semantic metadata (summaries, tags, complexity, cross-file edges) to emit per-batch GraphFragment JSON files for the knowledge graph pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS script run via Node; stores all authored metadata as inline constant maps (F for file nodes, SYM for function/class nodes, SEMANTIC for cross-file edges) rather than an external config file.

nodes.name: eval_samples.py
nodes.summary: Evaluation harness that runs the full bench-press CV pipeline (pose tracking, bar tracking, lift analysis) against every labeled sample clip and scores computed RPE vs ground-truth RPE encoded in each filename.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the evaluation loop: discovers video files, runs track_pose/track_bar/analyze_lift on each, compares computed RPE to ground-truth from the filename, saves overlay PNGs and JSON dumps, and prints a formatted results table.

nodes.name: ground_truth_rpe
nodes.summary: Extracts ground-truth RPE from a video filename by regex-matching 'RPE N' patterns, taking the last match to handle doubled tokens in filenames.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Python CV sidecar covering pose-based bar tracking, rep segmentation, effort-from-slowdown, and the filming guidance the analysis depends on.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned Python dependencies for the FastAPI CV sidecar: fastapi, uvicorn, mediapipe (pose), numpy/scipy, and python-multipart for video upload.

nodes.name: next-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Next.js ambient TypeScript type references; not edited by hand.

nodes.name: next.config.mjs

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs

nodes.name: globals.css

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js layout loading Space Grotesk, Inter, and JetBrains Mono fonts via next/font and the global CSS; wraps every route in the app.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported root layout component that injects font CSS variables onto the html element and renders children inside an antialiased body.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.ts
nodes.summary: Tailwind theme configuration defining the IRON LEDGER palette (ink/blue/cream aliases for black/electric-blue/chalk), custom fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter, JetBrains Mono), and content globs for the Next.js app.
nodes.languageNotes: Token names are intentionally aliased: ink=black, blue=electric-blue, cream=chalk-white â€” the display names diverge from the semantic color values per the project design contract.

nodes.name: tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
nodes.summary: TypeScript incremental build cache generated artifact; not source, not edited by hand.

nodes.name: toLbs
nodes.summary: Converts a weight value to pounds, returning the value unchanged when the unit is already lbs.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, authenticated route views, client components, and shared layout/navigation components that compose the IRON LEDGER athlete-facing interface.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js API route handlers that expose REST endpoints for authentication, AI chat, form-check video analysis, program management, nutrition generation, and session logging.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic for the app including AI client wrappers, authentication, database access, powerlifting calculations, block-periodization programming, CV-service integration, velocity-based effort estimation, and AI prompt templates.

layers.name: CV Service
layers.description: Independent Python FastAPI sidecar that performs MediaPipe-based pose estimation, bar-path tracking, and RPE calibration for powerlifting form analysis.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level configuration and build tooling files including Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and environment variable templates.

layers.name: Data & Scripts
layers.description: Database initialization script that creates and seeds the SQLite schema for users, programs, sessions, and nutrition entries.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Top-level project README covering setup, architecture overview, and usage instructions for IRON LEDGER.

layers.name: Tooling & Analysis Artifacts
layers.description: Understand-anything analysis pipeline artifacts, intermediate graph files, and internal tooling scripts used for codebase understanding â€” not part of the runtime application.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The root README is the authoritative map of IRON LEDGER â€” an AI-powered powerlifting coach that weaves together block periodization, CV-based form checking, and sport-specific nutrition planning. Reading it first gives you the mental model you need for every file that follows: the layered stack (Next.js 14, SQLite, Anthropic SDK, Python CV sidecar), the file map, and the key design decisions such as email-only auth, local-first storage, and dual-LLM support.

tour.title: Root Layout & Entry Point
tour.description: src/app/layout.tsx is the Next.js App Router root â€” it wraps every page, establishes the global font stack and dark-theme CSS variables, and mounts the root providers. This is the literal entry point the browser hits first, making it the right place to understand how the entire React tree is structured before drilling into individual features.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses a nested layout.tsx hierarchy: the root layout wraps every route, while group layouts like (app)/layout.tsx wrap only the authenticated sub-routes. Files inside a route group folder (parentheses) are grouped logically without affecting the URL path.

tour.title: Domain Types & Zod Schemas
tour.description: src/lib/types.ts is the single most-imported file in the codebase â€” 30 other modules depend on it. It defines every domain concept: AthleteProfile, TrainingProgram, FormCheck, CvResult, NutritionPlan, and ChatMessage. Because these types are inferred directly from Zod schemas via z.infer<>, they act as both compile-time contracts and runtime validators, giving the full stack a single source of truth for the data model.
tour.languageLesson: Combining TypeScript with Zod via z.infer<typeof schema> eliminates the need to write duplicate interface definitions. The Zod schema performs runtime validation (useful at API boundaries), and TypeScript infers the static type automatically â€” one definition, two guarantees.

tour.title: Database: Schema & Singleton
tour.description: src/lib/db.ts is the persistence backbone, depended on by 20 modules. It opens a single better-sqlite3 connection in WAL mode, then calls ensureSchema to create every table â€” athletes, session_logs, form_checks, velocity_log, bodyweight_logs, nutrition_plans â€” on first run. The addColumnIfMissing helper makes schema evolution idempotent, so the app migrates itself without a separate migration runner. The scripts/init-db.ts script simply imports getDb to trigger this bootstrap on a fresh install.
tour.languageLesson: better-sqlite3 uses a synchronous, blocking API rather than async/await. This is intentional for a local-only SQLite database: it eliminates callback complexity and is safe inside Next.js server components and API routes that run in isolated request contexts.

tour.title: Authentication & Session Guard
tour.description: src/lib/auth.ts is the second most-imported file (21 dependents). It implements a minimal cookie-based session: getOrCreateAthlete upserts an athlete row keyed by normalized email, setSession writes the athlete ID into an HTTP-only cookie, and requireSession enforces auth on every server component and API route by redirecting to /login if the cookie is absent or stale. This pattern means there are no passwords, JWTs, or external auth services â€” just a UUID in a cookie validated against the local SQLite database.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js server components can call redirect() and cookies() directly from the React tree â€” no middleware file required. requireSession is a plain async function that any server component can await, making auth enforcement composable and colocated with the data-fetching code.

tour.title: Sports-Science Math Engine
tour.description: src/lib/calculations.ts implements all the pure sports-science arithmetic that the rest of the app builds on. It computes BMR via Mifflin-St Jeor or Katch-McArdle, scales to TDEE, then derives macro targets (protein/fat/carbs) from the training phase. On the strength side it provides estimatedOneRM (blending Brzycki and Epley), rpePercent for the standard RPE-to-%1RM table, and suggestWeight to translate a target RPE into a working load. The rpeColor function also drives the visual RPEBadge component used throughout the UI.
tour.languageLesson: Blending two 1RM formulas (Brzycki for lower-rep sets, Epley for higher) reduces systematic error at both ends of the rep range. This is a common technique in sports-science software â€” each formula is accurate in a narrow range, so a blended estimator is more robust across the full 1â€“15 rep spectrum.

tour.title: AI Abstraction Layer
tour.description: src/lib/ai.ts is the provider-agnostic AI gateway used by every feature that calls an LLM. It exposes two functions â€” aiGenerate for full-response text and aiStream for server-sent-event streaming â€” and routes each call to either the Anthropic or Gemini backend depending on the LLM_PROVIDER environment variable. The safeParseJson helper strips markdown code fences from AI output before JSON.parse, which is essential because language models often wrap structured responses in triple-backtick blocks.
tour.languageLesson: The lazy singleton pattern for LLM clients (instantiate on first call, then cache) avoids paying the import cost on every request in a serverless environment. The LLM_PROVIDER env-var switch makes it trivial to A/B test providers or fall back when one is rate-limited â€” no code changes needed.

tour.title: Block Periodization Engine
tour.description: src/lib/programming.ts implements the algorithmic core of the coaching product. stagePlan maps an athlete's experience level to an accumulation, intensification, or peaking block configuration. extractLifterSets pulls logged sets from session history and computes e1RM for each using calculations.ts. adjustExercise is the most important function: given recent performance history and an RPE target, it advances load on a successful week, holds on a borderline week, and applies a deload on failure â€” all respecting the athlete's equipment unit (kg vs lb) via plate-step rounding.
tour.languageLesson: Block periodization divides a training cycle into distinct phases (accumulation â†’ intensification â†’ peaking), each with different volume and intensity targets. The algorithmic autoregulation in adjustExercise implements a simple decision tree rather than calling the LLM for every weight update, keeping latency low and the logic auditable.

tour.title: Onboarding: Profile to First Program
tour.description: The onboarding flow is where the app generates its first AI artifact. src/app/onboarding/OnboardingWizard.tsx walks the athlete through five steps â€” personal info, current 1RMs, schedule preferences, dietary restrictions, and a summary â€” before POSTing to src/app/api/onboarding/route.ts. That API handler validates the payload with Zod, estimates novice maxes if no lifts are provided (using calculations.ts), upserts the athlete row, then calls aiGenerate with the program prompt to produce the athlete's first block-periodization program and stores it in SQLite.

tour.title: Training Program View & Auto-Adjust
tour.description: src/app/(app)/program/ProgramView.tsx is the primary training interface, rendering the AI-generated weekly schedule as collapsible day cards with per-exercise sets, reps, RPE targets, and AI-adjusted weight suggestions. Each week tab triggers a call to src/app/api/program/adjust/route.ts, which algorithmically updates exercise weights based on logged session history using the adjustExercise logic from programming.ts â€” no second AI call required. The LogSessionModal embedded in this view lets athletes record actual performance set-by-set, closing the feedback loop.

tour.title: CV Sidecar: Pose & Bar Tracking
tour.description: The Python CV sidecar is a self-contained FastAPI service in cv-service/. cv-service/app.py exposes /analyze and /health endpoints; when a video arrives, it calls cv-service/pose.py to run MediaPipe BlazePose and produce a PoseTrack (per-frame landmark XY arrays), then cv-service/bar.py to track the bar using CSRT optical flow seeded from wrist landmarks with Hough circle refinement. The cv-service/README.md documents the filming requirements the algorithm depends on â€” side-on camera angle, unobstructed barbell plates â€” which are critical constraints for getting useful analysis.
tour.languageLesson: CSRT (Channel and Spatial Reliability Tracking) is a discriminative correlation filter tracker that is more accurate than KCF but slower. The dual-pass strategy (forward + backward tracking, merged with Hough detections) helps recover from frames where the tracker drifts due to occlusion or plate blur.

tour.title: Honest RPE: Velocity-Based Effort
tour.description: Two modules implement the 'honest RPE' system described in the README. cv-service/analysis.py is the core analysis engine: it segments the video into individual reps using the Calgary Barbell phase model (unrack/descent/pause/press/lockout), measures concentric rep time for each, and computes velocity loss across the set to estimate RIR. On the TypeScript side, src/lib/velocity.ts applies per-lifter linear regression calibration when sufficient history exists, refining the generic lift-specific loss curves with the athlete's own data logged via the /api/formcheck/calibrate endpoint.
tour.languageLesson: Velocity-based training uses the observation that rep speed decreases predictably as fatigue accumulates within a set. Measuring the percentage drop from the fastest rep to the slowest maps reliably to Reps-in-Reserve (RIR). Per-lifter calibration via linear regression personalizes this curve because individual fatigue response varies significantly.

tour.title: Form Check: Full Pipeline
tour.description: The form-check feature integrates every prior system. src/app/(app)/formcheck/FormCheckClient.tsx provides the upload modal (video + lift metadata), then POSTs to src/app/api/formcheck/route.ts. That handler calls cvService.ts to dispatch the video to the Python sidecar, retrieves the athlete's velocity calibration history, estimates RPE via velocity.ts, then calls aiGenerate with prompts from src/lib/prompts/formcheck.ts â€” which embeds the CV bar-path table, per-phase findings, and lift-specific cues â€” to produce structured coaching feedback. The result is stored and rendered as a form-check card with bar-path overlay, rep metrics table, and inline RPE calibration control.

tour.title: AI Chat Coach
tour.description: src/app/api/chat/route.ts implements the streaming coaching conversation. Before calling the LLM it assembles rich athlete context â€” profile, current training week, and recent form check summaries â€” using src/lib/prompts/chat.ts, then calls aiStream to pipe server-sent events directly back to the browser. src/app/(app)/chat/ChatView.tsx consumes the SSE stream and renders messages incrementally with auto-scroll and quick-action chips. Both the user message and the completed assistant response are persisted to SQLite, so chat history survives page reloads.
tour.languageLesson: Server-Sent Events (SSE) are simpler than WebSockets for unidirectional server-to-client streaming: the client opens a normal HTTP connection and the server writes 'data: ...' lines. Next.js Route Handlers support streaming via ReadableStream, and the Anthropic SDK's stream() method emits chunks that can be forwarded directly to the client.

tour.title: Nutrition & Progress Tracking
tour.description: The final two features complete the athlete's data loop. src/app/(app)/nutrition/NutritionView.tsx displays deterministic macro targets computed by calculations.ts and offers a button to generate an AI meal plan via src/app/api/nutrition/generate/route.ts, which embeds the calculated macros and dietary restrictions in the prompt from src/lib/prompts/nutrition.ts. src/app/(app)/progress/ProgressView.tsx visualizes the long-term training record as Recharts dashboards: e1RM trend per lift (computed from every logged session), a 7-day rolling bodyweight average, and weekly tonnage â€” giving the athlete quantitative evidence of their adaptation over time.
